title: Project skeleton, pinned deps & lint gate
description:
## Goal
A green-building crate skeleton with the strict AGENTS.md lint gate and pinned dependencies.

## Scope
- In: Cargo.toml deps pinned (agent-client-protocol 0.13.x +unstable, -tokio, tokio, reqwest+reqwest-eventsource, serde/serde_json, ignore/grep/globset, uuid, tracing(+subscriber), clap, thiserror; dev: test-log, proptest); crate-root lints (#![forbid(unsafe_code)], deny pedantic, no unwrap/expect/panic/todo); clap `serve` subcommand stub; tracing init.
- Out: any ACP/DeepSeek behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo build/test/fmt --check/clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings all pass on the skeleton.
2. `cargo doc --no-deps` has no undocumented public API.
3. `serve` subcommand exists and exits cleanly.

## Testing
- A trivial passing test; CI-equivalent gate runs locally.

## Notes
- Blocks all other tasks. Run `cargo audit` before adding deps.
close_reason: Implemented green skeleton with pinned deps, strict lint gate, serve subcommand, docs, and a passing test. Kept direct tokio runtime dependency but did not add agent-client-protocol-tokio because the only published helper is 0.11.1 and pulls duplicate ACP 0.11 types.

title: Local tools: list_dir, glob, grep
description:
## Goal
Local code-navigation tools the ACP client fs surface does not provide.

## Scope
- In: list_dir(path), glob(pattern) via globset, grep(regex) via ignore+grep (ripgrep engine), respecting .gitignore; bounded/truncated results with a note when capped.
- Out: client-side execution (these are always local).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. grep returns matches with file:line; glob matches expected paths; list_dir lists entries.
2. Results are bounded and signal truncation.

## Testing
- Temp-dir fixtures; assert matches, ignore handling, truncation note.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop. Parallel with read_file.
close_reason: Added local list_dir, glob, and grep tools with gitignore-aware filtering and truncation

title: DeepSeek-backed coding agent over ACP
description:
## Problem Statement

ACP-speaking editors (Zed, neovim/CodeCompanion) can drive Claude and Codex as coding agents, but not DeepSeek. DeepSeek exposes only a chat-completions API (tool-calling + streaming) with no agentic engine, so no existing adapter turns it into a full ACP coding agent. The one DeepSeek+ACP precedent (CodeWhale) is TUI-first and exposes only text prompt/response over ACP — tool-backed editing and permissions are not surfaced.

## Solution

A headless, ACP-first adapter binary that presents DeepSeek V4 as a coding agent to any ACP client. We build the agent harness ourselves (system prompt, tool catalog, the prompt→tool-call→execute→feed-back loop, conversation history, permission gating) on top of DeepSeek's streaming API, and crucially surface tool calls, edits and permission prompts THROUGH ACP — the part prior art skipped. The editor is the only UI.

## Architecture

Two independent channels. Editor⇄adapter: JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, handled by the agent-client-protocol crate (the official rust-sdk). Adapter⇄DeepSeek: HTTPS + SSE to the OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint, via a thin client we own. The adapter is the translator + agent loop in the middle. A translation boundary keeps ACP/HTTP types out of the harness core (AGENTS.md: business logic must not depend on I/O).

## Scope (features / milestones)

Tracer-bullet sequence, each demoable in a real editor: M0 connects (streaming text), M1 reads (read-only tool loop), M2 edits + permissions (= v0.1 release line), M3 runs (hybrid shell), M4 polish (model picker, images, docs).

## Out of Scope

No TUI. No MCP client support, no loadSession, no auto model-router, no apply_patch-style edits in v0.1.

## Key Constraints

Strict AGENTS.md: forbid unsafe; clippy pedantic; no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; minimal audited deps; documented public APIs; never log secrets.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: Dev CLI: drive the agent over stdio for e2e testing
description:
## Goal
A developer harness to exercise the agent without an editor.

## Scope
- In: a hidden `dev` subcommand (or test bin) that pipes JSON-RPC frames to the running agent over stdio and prints responses/updates; a scripted initialize→new→prompt smoke flow.
- Out: a real TUI.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Running the dev CLI against the agent completes an initialize→session/new→session/prompt round-trip and prints streamed updates.

## Testing
- Smoke: dev CLI drives a prompt end-to-end (mock or real backend behind a flag).

## Notes
- Depends on the prompt turn. Priority 2 (tooling aid).
close_reason: Implemented hidden dev stdio smoke flow

title: M3: Shell command execution (hybrid terminal)
description:
## Problem Statement

Many coding tasks need to run commands (build, test, git). Zed offers terminal callbacks; CodeCompanion does not advertise terminal capability — so a single mechanism will not work across both targets.

## Solution

Add run_command: use client terminal/* when advertised (Zed), fall back to local tokio::process when not (CodeCompanion), permission-gated like other mutations.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed user, I want the agent to run commands in the editor terminal, so that I see output natively.
2. As a CodeCompanion user, I want commands to still run (locally), so that the agent works despite no terminal capability.
3. As a user, I want to approve commands, so that nothing destructive runs without consent.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given clientCapabilities.terminal, when run_command executes, then it uses terminal/create + output/wait_for_exit/release and surfaces output.
2. Given no terminal capability, then it runs via local tokio::process and reports output through tool_call_update.
3. Given run_command in ask/accept-edits mode, then it requires permission; cancellation kills the child process.

## Implementation Notes

- Capability detection chooses terminal vs local at runtime. Cancellation aborts running child processes.

## Testing Notes

- Both paths (terminal advertised vs not); cancellation kills the child.

## Out of Scope

Background/long-running terminal session management beyond a single command; model picker; images.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: session/cancel: abort SSE & flush, return cancelled
description:
## Goal
Real cancellation that aborts the in-flight DeepSeek stream.

## Scope
- In: cancellation token plumbed through the turn; session/cancel aborts the live SSE request, flushes pending session/update, returns StopReason::cancelled (not an error).
- Out: cancelling child processes (M3).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given an in-flight turn, session/cancel aborts the SSE stream promptly.
2. Pending updates are flushed before session/prompt returns StopReason::cancelled.

## Testing
- Mock LlmClient that streams slowly; cancel mid-stream; assert cancelled + flushed updates + no error surfaced.

## Notes
- Depends on the prompt turn.
close_reason: Implemented session/cancel with per-turn cancellation tokens, prompt response cancellation semantics, and regression coverage for flushed partial updates.

title: Adapter does not expose shell or file-edit capabilities
description:
When using deepseek-acp-adapter, the agent appears to lack both shell execution and file editing capabilities.

Observed:
- The adapter/client reports no shell capability.
- The adapter/client reports no edit-file capability.
- This prevents normal coding-agent workflows that require inspecting the workspace and applying changes.

Expected:
- The ACP capability negotiation and tool registry should expose supported shell execution and file-edit tools, or clearly fail with an explicit unsupported-capability message/configuration issue.

Investigation notes:
- Check capability advertisement during session initialization.
- Check tool registration/mapping for shell execution.
- Check tool registration/mapping for file edits.
- Verify request routing from ACP tool calls to the underlying implementation.

Acceptance criteria:
- A client session using deepseek-acp-adapter can invoke shell commands when enabled.
- A client session using deepseek-acp-adapter can edit files when enabled.
- If either capability is intentionally disabled, the adapter reports a clear actionable reason instead of silently omitting it.
close_reason: Implemented adapter-owned write_file, edit_file, and run_command tools with permission gating and ACP tool-kind reporting.

title: Tool registry + agentic tool-call loop
description:
## Goal
Generalize the turn into a tool-call loop with reporting and a safety cap.

## Scope
- In: tool JSON-schema registry passed in the DeepSeek tools param; accumulate streamed tool_call args by index; dispatch; report tool_call(pending)+tool_call_update(completed/failed); feed results back; iterate until no tool calls or iteration cap (default ~25) → StopReason::max_turn_requests.
- Out: concrete tools (separate tasks).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Streamed tool_calls are reassembled correctly and dispatched.
2. Each call emits tool_call then tool_call_update; results are appended to history and sent back to the model.
3. The iteration cap trips returning max_turn_requests.

## Testing
- Mock model emitting fragmented tool_calls across deltas; assert reassembly, update sequence, cap behavior.

## Notes
- Core M1 infra. Depends on the M0 prompt turn.
close_reason: Implemented tool-call streaming assembly, ACP tool reporting, registry execution feedback, and max-turn-request cap coverage.

title: Use ACP client fs/write_text_file when available
description:
Audit finding: read_file uses client fs/read_text_file when the client advertises it, but write_file and edit_file write directly to local disk and never call fs/write_text_file when clientCapabilities.fs.writeTextFile is true. ACP v1 filesystem methods exist to preserve client/editor state and let clients track modifications.

Acceptance criteria:
- write_file uses fs/write_text_file when the client advertises writeTextFile and a connection is available.
- edit_file reads via fs/read_text_file when available and writes via fs/write_text_file when available.
- Local filesystem fallback remains available when client fs capabilities are absent.
- Tests cover client fs write routing and local fallback.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add optional ACP session lifecycle methods
description:
Audit finding: ACP v1 includes optional session/list, session/load, session/resume, session/close, session_info_update, and logout flows gated by capabilities. This adapter does not advertise or implement these methods. That is currently allowed if unadvertised, but codex-acp implements logout, load, list, and close and can serve as a reference.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide which optional lifecycle capabilities this adapter should support in early development.
- Implement chosen handlers and advertise only implemented capabilities.
- For unsupported optional methods, document the intentional omission in README or issue notes.
- Tests cover advertised capability bits and handler behavior.

title: DeepSeek LlmClient trait + reqwest/SSE impl
description:
## Goal
A thin streaming DeepSeek client behind an LlmClient trait (the mock seam).

## Scope
- In: LlmClient trait; reqwest + reqwest-eventsource impl of /chat/completions stream:true; env config (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/BASE_URL/MODEL, default deepseek-v4-pro); parse text + reasoning_content deltas; clear error if key missing; never log the key.
- Out: tool-call parsing (M1), images (M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given canned SSE, the client yields ordered text + reasoning deltas and a finish reason.
2. Missing API key returns a clear typed error; the key never appears in tracing output.

## Testing
- Mock transport / fixture SSE; assert delta ordering and finish mapping; assert no key in logs.

## Notes
- Depends on skeleton. Can run in parallel with initialize.
close_reason: Implemented DeepSeek client trait and streaming SSE parsing

title: Improve test coverage for ACP bootstrap
description: Expand coverage around the ACP adapter bootstrap and handshake path. Focus on initialize/authenticate handling, client-capability capture, and any remaining edge cases in the DeepSeek client seam.
close_reason: Expanded ACP bootstrap and DeepSeek seam coverage with deterministic request-handler tests and parser edge cases.

title: README & setup docs (env config + GUI env gotcha + client snippets)
description:
## Goal
First-run docs so the adapter is usable in Zed and CodeCompanion.

## Scope
- In: README covering env-only config (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/BASE_URL/MODEL), the GUI env-inheritance gotcha (use agent_servers.env in Zed), Zed and CodeCompanion config snippets, supported tools/modes, and current limitations (no MCP/loadSession).
- Out: published website.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. README documents config, the GUI env gotcha workaround, and working Zed + CodeCompanion setup snippets.

## Testing
- Manual: following the README configures the agent in both editors.

## Notes
- Capstone; depends on model selection + image input so the full feature set is documented.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: read_file tool (client fs + local fallback)
description:
## Goal
Let the model read files, preferring the editor's buffer-aware fs.

## Scope
- In: read_file(path, optional line range); route to client fs/read_text_file when fs.readTextFile is advertised, else local read; bounded output.
- Out: writing/editing.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With fs.readTextFile advertised, reads go through the client (respecting unsaved buffers).
2. Without it, a local read is used. Line ranges honored.

## Testing
- Fake client advertising/not advertising fs; assert routing and content.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop.
close_reason: Implemented read_file tool with client fs routing and local fallback

title: Emit optional ACP plan and slash-command updates
description:
Audit finding: ACP v1 supports plan updates and available_commands_update notifications. This adapter currently emits thought/message/tool updates only. These features are optional, but useful for client UX and codex-acp uses them for plans and builtin commands.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide which slash commands and plan updates are useful for the DeepSeek adapter.
- Emit available_commands_update after session creation/load when commands exist.
- Convert model/planning signals into SessionUpdate::Plan when feasible.
- Tests cover emitted notification shape and command prompt handling.

title: M0: Protocol bootstrap & streaming text chat
description:
## Problem Statement

Nothing yet lets an ACP editor talk to DeepSeek. We need the end-to-end pipe working: editor handshake → prompt → live streamed DeepSeek answer.

## Solution

Implement the ACP Agent trait for the initialize/session lifecycle and a text-only streaming turn: session/prompt calls DeepSeek with stream:true and forwards token deltas as session/update chunks, maps reasoning_content to thought chunks, and honors cancellation.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed/CodeCompanion user, I want to open a session and send a prompt, so that DeepSeek's answer streams live into my editor.
2. As a user, I want reasoning shown separately from the answer, so that I can follow the model's thinking.
3. As a user, I want to cancel a running turn, so that I can stop a long or wrong answer immediately.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a client connects, when it calls initialize, then the agent negotiates protocol version and advertises capabilities (no auth method; prompt caps text+embeddedContext+image; loadSession false) and stores the client's capabilities.
2. Given a new session, when session/prompt is sent, then DeepSeek is called with stream:true and text deltas arrive as agent_message_chunk updates.
3. Given the model returns reasoning_content, then it is forwarded as agent_thought_chunk updates.
4. Given a turn in flight, when session/cancel arrives, then the live SSE request is aborted and the turn returns StopReason::cancelled after flushing pending updates.
5. Given the model finishes, then session/prompt returns StopReason::end_turn (or max_tokens).

## Implementation Notes

- Depend on agent-client-protocol (0.13.x pinned, unstable feature) + -tokio; tokio multi-thread runtime.
- Thin DeepSeek client (reqwest + reqwest-eventsource); model/base_url/key from env; never log the key.
- Translation boundary; LlmClient trait is the mock seam.
- Cancellation via a tokio cancellation token that aborts the SSE stream.

## Testing Notes

- Mock LlmClient feeding canned SSE deltas; in-memory fake ACP Client capturing updates. Cover all returned StopReasons including cancelled.

## Out of Scope

Tools, permissions, editing, shell, model picker; images are declared in caps but not exercised here.
close_reason: M0 acceptance is implemented: initialize handshake, DeepSeek streaming prompt turns, reasoning chunks, finish reasons, and cancellation are covered by closed child tasks. The remaining dev CLI child is a tooling aid and stays open separately.

title: Use ACP terminal methods for command tools
description:
Audit finding: run_command executes locally with std::process::Command and reports text output, but ACP v1 terminal methods allow terminal/create, output, wait_for_exit, kill, release, and terminal content embedded in tool calls when clientCapabilities.terminal is true.

Acceptance criteria:
- run_command uses terminal/create + wait/output/release when the client advertises terminal support.
- Tool calls can include terminal content so clients can display live output.
- Local process execution remains a fallback when terminal support is absent.
- Cancellation attempts to stop active terminal/process work.
- Tests cover terminal-capability routing and fallback behavior.
close_reason: Implemented ACP terminal methods for run_command with client terminal fallback

title: Audit ACP v1 protocol feature coverage
description:
Audit deepseek-acp-adapter against the full Agent Client Protocol v1 documentation.

Scope:
1. Start at https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/v1/overview and review every linked v1 protocol page that explains ACP behavior, methods, capabilities, session lifecycle, prompts, tool calls, filesystem, terminal, authentication, errors, and any unstable/optional features documented under v1.
2. For each ACP v1 feature, determine whether this adapter implements it, partially implements it, intentionally omits it, or is missing it unexpectedly.
3. When useful, inspect the codex-acp codebase as a reference implementation to see how another adapter handles the same feature.

Deliverable:
- A coverage matrix or checklist mapping ACP v1 features/pages to current implementation status.
- File/function references for implemented or partial features.
- A list of gaps split into required protocol compliance, useful optional support, and intentionally unsupported features.
- Follow-up beads for any concrete implementation gaps discovered.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every ACP v1 documentation page reachable from the v1 overview is accounted for.
- Each feature has an implementation status and supporting evidence from this repository.
- codex-acp is consulted for at least ambiguous or non-obvious features, or the task notes why it was not needed/available.
- Any missing required feature has a proposed implementation task.
close_reason: Completed ACP v1 coverage audit, documented matrix in comments, and created follow-up beads for required and optional gaps.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text:
ACP v1 coverage audit completed.

Docs reviewed:
- https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/v1/overview
- initialization, authentication, session-setup, session-list, prompt-turn, content, tool-calls, file-system, terminals, agent-plan, session-modes, session-config-options, slash-commands, extensibility, transports, schema

Reference checked:
- /home/lotso/code/codex-acp
- codex-acp registers initialize/authenticate/logout/session new/load/list/close/prompt/cancel/set_mode/set_model/set_config_option in src/codex_agent.rs.
- codex-acp advertises load_session, auth.logout, mcp.http, session close/list, and emits plan, terminal, config, and available command updates in src/thread.rs.

Coverage matrix:
- Overview / JSON-RPC / stdio transport: implemented through agent-client-protocol Agent builder and Stdio transport. No custom transport support. OK for stdio baseline.
- initialize: partial. Client capabilities are stored and agentCapabilities are returned, but response echoes requested protocol version and does not include agentInfo. PromptCapabilities currently advertise image + embeddedContext even though prompt parsing accepts only text.
- authenticate: implemented as empty success. OK because authMethods is empty and no auth is required. logout is not implemented or advertised.
- session/new: partial. Creates sessions, validates absolute cwd/additionalDirectories, returns modes and configOptions. Does not process mcpServers.
- session/load, session/resume, session/close, session/list: not implemented and not advertised. Allowed when unadvertised, but useful optional gaps.
- additionalDirectories: partially implemented but not advertised via sessionCapabilities.additionalDirectories.
- MCP servers: missing required stdio support from ACP v1 session setup; no MCP server connection or tool exposure.
- session/prompt: implemented for text-only prompts; history, active-turn guard, streaming LLM output, tool loop, and cancellation token exist.
- content blocks: partial. Text supported. ResourceLink baseline and advertised image/embedded resource support are not actually accepted.
- session/update message/thought chunks: implemented. User message replay for load not applicable because load is unsupported.
- tool calls: partial. ToolCall + ToolCallUpdate final status/content/rawOutput are emitted with correct kinds for built-in tools. No in_progress update, locations, diff content, or terminal content.
- session/request_permission: implemented for mutating tools with allow/reject once/always and cancelled handling. allow_always is cached per session/tool name.
- file system client methods: partial. fs/read_text_file is used when client advertises it. fs/write_text_file is not used; writes go to local disk.
- terminal client methods: not implemented. run_command uses local std::process::Command and plain text output instead of terminal/create/output/wait/kill/release.
- agent plan: not implemented. Optional.
- session modes: implemented. session/set_mode and initial modes are supported.
- session config options: implemented. mode/model/reasoning selectors returned and session/set_config_option returns complete config state. Agent-originated config_option_update is not implemented.
- slash commands: not implemented. Optional.
- session_info_update: not implemented. Optional tied to session/list/lifecycle.
- extensibility/_meta/custom methods: no custom extensions advertised; schema crate carries meta fields where used.
- schema conformance: mostly delegated to agent-client-protocol crate. Implementation-level gaps are tracked below.

Follow-up beads created:
- daa-u8j Support ACP v1 MCP stdio servers (required/high)
- daa-lpi Fix ACP v1 initialization negotiation and capabilities (required/high)
- daa-vb2 Restore max_turn_requests guard in prompt loop (required/high)
- daa-bzw Use ACP client fs/write_text_file when available (optional/useful)
- daa-gk3 Use ACP terminal methods for command tools (optional/useful)
- daa-cc3 Add optional ACP session lifecycle methods (optional/useful)
- daa-f9x Emit optional ACP plan and slash-command updates (optional/useful)
- daa-ud0 Advertise additionalDirectories session capability (optional/useful but should match current behavior)

Conclusion:
The adapter covers the minimum handshake/session/prompt/tool-call skeleton, but it is not yet fully ACP v1-compliant because MCP stdio support, prompt content capability alignment, and max_turn_requests handling need fixes.

title: Make prompt turn request limit configurable
description:
The adapter currently uses a local MAX_TURN_REQUESTS constant for ACP prompt/tool loops. ACP defines the max_turn_requests stop reason but does not mandate a fixed numeric limit. Make the limit configurable while preserving a sensible default.

Possible design: add a serve CLI option such as --max-turn-requests <N> and thread it into PromptTurnEnvironment/session handling. Other config surfaces are acceptable if they fit the current CLI architecture better.

Acceptance criteria:
- The default prompt turn request limit remains documented and tested.
- Users can configure the limit at startup, likely via serve --max-turn-requests <N>.
- Invalid values are rejected with a clear CLI/config error.
- Exceeding the configured limit returns ACP StopReason::MaxTurnRequests.
- Tests cover default behavior, custom limit behavior, and invalid configuration.

title: M2: File editing with permissions over ACP (v0.1)
description:
## Problem Statement

The whole point of the adapter: let DeepSeek edit code with the user in control. Prior DeepSeek+ACP art does not surface edits or permissions through ACP. This feature is the v0.1 release line.

## Solution

Add write_file and edit_file (string-replace) via client fs, gated by session/request_permission, with permission-posture modes (ask / accept-edits / yolo) switchable via session/set_mode. Edits render as native diffs in the editor.

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want the agent to edit files, so that it can implement changes.
2. As a user, I want to approve or reject each edit, so that I stay in control.
3. As a user, I want to choose a posture (ask / accept-edits / yolo), so that I trade safety for speed.
4. As a user, I want edits shown as diffs, so that I can review them in my editor.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a mutating tool in ask mode, when the agent wants to run it, then it first calls session/request_permission offering allow_once/allow_always/reject_once/reject_always and proceeds only on allow.
2. Given allow_always for a tool, then later calls of that tool in the same session are not re-prompted.
3. Given edit_file, then the file is read (via client fs) first, the exact old_string is replaced, and the result written via fs/write_text_file (native diff shown); a missing or non-unique old_string fails the tool with a clear error fed back to the model.
4. Given mode=accept-edits edits auto-allow but shell still prompts; mode=yolo auto-allows all; session/set_mode switches posture live.

## Implementation Notes

- String-replace edit (NOT apply_patch) — DeepSeek is weaker at exact patch syntax; the write still goes through client fs so the editor renders the diff.
- Permission memory keyed per-tool per-session.
- Modes advertised in session/new (currentModeId/availableModes).

## Testing Notes

- proptest the string-replace (apply-then-read-back invariants); no-match/ambiguous-match cases. Permission gating + allow_always memory; mode posture matrix.

## Out of Scope

Shell/terminal, model picker, image input, MCP.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: Fix ACP v1 initialization negotiation and capabilities
description:
Audit finding: build_initialize_response currently echoes the requested protocol version and advertises image + embeddedContext prompt capabilities even though prompt parsing accepts only text blocks. ACP v1 requires agents and clients to agree on a supported protocol version, and advertised prompt capabilities must match accepted content.

Scope:
- Respond with the adapter's latest supported protocol version instead of blindly echoing unsupported versions.
- Add agentInfo/name/version where available.
- Either support advertised image/resource content or stop advertising those capabilities.
- Ensure baseline Text and ResourceLink support is handled correctly per ACP v1.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initialize response negotiates only supported protocol versions.
- PromptCapabilities exactly match what session/prompt accepts.
- Text and ResourceLink prompt blocks are supported or ResourceLink rejection is tracked with a justified compliance decision.
- Tests cover unsupported/newer protocol input and each advertised prompt content type.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Remove fixed prompt turn cap that ends long tool conversations
description:
Observed behavior:
- Long conversations with repeated model-requested tool calls stop with ACP stopReason=max_turn_requests.
- Example log from 2026-06-04:
  DEBUG connection{name="deepseek-acp-adapter"}: ... Response { id: Number(3), method: "session/prompt", response: Ok(Object {"stopReason": String("max_turn_requests")}) }
- This prevents the user from running conversations to completion when the model legitimately needs more than the adapter's local cap.

Current deepseek-acp-adapter findings:
- src/main.rs defines MAX_TURN_REQUESTS as 25.
- run_prompt_turn iterates for _ in 0..MAX_TURN_REQUESTS and sets StopReason::MaxTurnRequests if every iteration requested tools.
- The test prompt_tool_loop_stops_at_turn_cap currently locks in this behavior by expecting StopReason::MaxTurnRequests and exactly MAX_TURN_REQUESTS client requests.

Reference implementation checked locally:
- /home/lotso/code/codex-acp/src/codex_agent.rs: prompt gets the session thread and awaits thread.prompt(request), then returns PromptResponse::new(stop_reason).
- /home/lotso/code/codex-acp/src/thread.rs: handle_prompt submits a Codex Op, records a PromptState, and returns a response receiver.
- /home/lotso/code/codex-acp/src/thread.rs: PromptState resolves on terminal Codex events such as TurnComplete, TurnAborted, ShutdownComplete, or Error. There is no equivalent fixed local ACP turn counter that returns max_turn_requests after N tool calls.

Expected behavior:
- The adapter should allow a prompt to continue until the model reaches a terminal finish reason, the user cancels, the backend errors, or a real externally configured runtime/resource guard fires.
- Do not return StopReason::MaxTurnRequests merely because the prompt required more than 25 model/tool iterations.
- If a guard is still needed for safety, make it explicit/configurable and high enough not to break normal long-running agent conversations; prefer cancellation/resource controls over a hidden fixed turn cap.

Acceptance criteria:
- Long tool-call conversations can exceed 25 model/tool iterations without returning max_turn_requests.
- Existing cancellation behavior still returns StopReason::Cancelled promptly.
- Backend terminal finish reasons still map to the correct ACP stop reasons.
- Tests are updated so the former turn-cap test covers the new unlimited/configurable behavior instead of expecting MaxTurnRequests.
- Add regression coverage for more than 25 consecutive tool-call turns and verify session history is preserved.
close_reason: Already implemented: run_prompt_turn uses an infinite loop with no fixed turn cap; regression test prompt_tool_loop_continues_beyond_25_turns validates >25 consecutive tool-call turns

title: run_command via client terminal/* when advertised (Zed)
description:
## Goal
Host commands in the editor's terminal when the client supports it.

## Scope
- In: when clientCapabilities.terminal is advertised, run_command uses terminal/create + terminal/output + wait_for_exit + release; capability detection selects this over the local path.
- Out: persistent/background terminals.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With terminal advertised, run_command uses the terminal/* methods and surfaces output/exit.
2. Without it, falls back to the local path (unchanged).

## Testing
- Fake client advertising terminal; assert terminal/* call sequence; assert fallback when absent.

## Notes
- Depends on the local shell task (shares the run_command tool).
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: run_command via local process (permission-gated, cancellable)
description:
## Goal
Run shell commands locally so the agent works on clients without terminal capability (CodeCompanion).

## Scope
- In: run_command(cmd) via tokio::process; capture stdout/stderr/exit; permission-gated; reported via tool_call_update; cancellation kills the child.
- Out: editor-hosted terminal (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With no terminal capability, run_command executes locally and reports output + exit code.
2. session/cancel kills the running child process.

## Testing
- Run a deterministic command; assert output/exit; cancel a long command and assert the child is killed.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate. Extends cancellation (M0) to child processes.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: Model selection: unstable set_model + availableModels (+ env default)
description:
## Goal
Let capable clients pick the model in-session; everyone else uses the env default.

## Scope
- In: advertise availableModels (v4-flash, v4-pro, v4-pro+thinking) and handle session/set_model behind the crate's unstable feature; DEEPSEEK_MODEL is the initial/default; graceful no-op for clients lacking the API.
- Out: auto model-router.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A client supporting set_model can switch the active model mid-session; the next DeepSeek call uses it.
2. A client without it runs on DEEPSEEK_MODEL (default deepseek-v4-pro) with no error.

## Testing
- Fake clients with/without set_model; assert switching vs default.

## Notes
- Depends on the M0 prompt turn (independent of tools).
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: edit_file string-replace tool (+ proptest)
description:
## Goal
Reliable targeted edits via exact string replacement.

## Scope
- In: edit_file(path, old_string, new_string): read via client fs, replace the unique exact occurrence, write via client fs; error clearly on no-match or non-unique match; result fed back to the model.
- Out: apply_patch/unified-diff format (explicitly out per plan).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A unique old_string is replaced and written via client fs (native diff).
2. Absent or ambiguous (non-unique) old_string fails with a descriptive error; the file is unchanged.

## Testing
- proptest: apply-then-read-back invariants; unit tests for no-match and ambiguous-match.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate and read_file. String-replace, not patch (DeepSeek is weaker at patch syntax).
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: session/new + streaming text prompt turn
description:
## Goal
A text-only prompt turn: session/prompt streams DeepSeek output to the editor.

## Scope
- In: session/new (in-memory session: history, cwd, mode); session/prompt → call DeepSeek (stream) → forward text as agent_message_chunk, reasoning as agent_thought_chunk; append to history; return StopReason end_turn/max_tokens.
- Out: tools, permissions, cancellation (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. session/new returns a session id and advertised modes.
2. A prompt streams agent_message_chunk updates; reasoning maps to thought chunks; turn returns end_turn (or max_tokens).
3. Conversation history is retained per session and replayed on the next prompt.

## Testing
- Mock LlmClient + fake ACP client; assert update sequence, StopReason, and history replay.

## Notes
- Depends on initialize + DeepSeek client.
close_reason: Implemented in-memory ACP sessions, streamed DeepSeek prompt turns, and history replay tests.

title: Expose ACP session options for model effort and thought level
description:
In Neovim, `acp_session_options` exposes controls for model/session settings such as model effort and thought level. Those controls work with Codex ACP, but the `deepseek_acp_adapter` session only exposes permission modes, so the user cannot change model effort, thought level, or related model/session options from the ACP client.

Observed behavior:
- Typing `acp_session_options` in Neovim shows the adapter does not provide controls for effort/thought-level/model settings.
- The adapter can stream DeepSeek reasoning content, but there is no ACP session configuration surface for choosing reasoning behavior.

Expected behavior:
- The adapter should advertise and handle ACP session config/model options that Neovim can render in `acp_session_options`.
- At minimum, expose model-related options that map cleanly to DeepSeek request parameters, such as reasoning/thinking effort or thought level if supported by the selected model/API.
- Setting an option from the client should update the session state used for later prompts and return updated config options when the ACP protocol expects it.

Reference implementation checked locally:
- `/home/lotso/code/codex-acp/src/codex_agent.rs`
  - registers handlers for `SetSessionModelRequest` and `SetSessionConfigOptionRequest` in addition to `SetSessionModeRequest`.
  - `new_session` and `load_session` return `.models(load.models)` and `.config_options(load.config_options)`.
  - `set_session_config_option` calls `thread.set_config_option(...)` and returns `SetSessionConfigOptionResponse::new(config_options)`.
- `/home/lotso/code/codex-acp/src/thread.rs`
  - builds `SessionConfigOption::select("model", ...)` with category `Model`.
  - builds `SessionConfigOption::select("reasoning_effort", ...)` with category `ThoughtLevel` when the current model preset supports multiple efforts.
  - handles config ids `mode`, `model`, and `reasoning_effort`.
  - also exposes `SessionModelState` with model IDs encoded as `model/effort` and handles `SetSessionModelRequest`.

Current adapter findings:
- `src/main.rs` registers `InitializeRequest`, `AuthenticateRequest`, `NewSessionRequest`, `SetSessionModeRequest`, `PromptRequest`, and cancel handling, but no `SetSessionModelRequest` or `SetSessionConfigOptionRequest` handlers.
- `handle_new_session_request` returns `NewSessionResponse::new(session_id).modes(default_session_modes())` only.
- `build_initialize_response` advertises prompt/auth/load-session capabilities only; there is no model/config option path.

Acceptance criteria:
- `acp_session_options` in Neovim shows relevant model/session settings for this adapter.
- The adapter accepts ACP requests to change those settings and applies them to subsequent DeepSeek chat/completions requests.
- Unsupported options are rejected with clear `invalid_params` errors rather than silently ignored.
- Add tests covering advertised options, option updates, and prompt behavior using the updated session settings.
close_reason: Implemented ACP session config options for model and reasoning effort, wired updates into DeepSeek requests, and added regression tests

title: Raise deepseek-acp-adapter test coverage to 90%+
description:
## Goal
Increase automated test coverage for the existing deepseek-acp-adapter codebase to at least 90%.

## Scope
- Add focused tests for currently untested or lightly tested paths in src/lib.rs and src/main.rs.
- Prefer small, behavior-oriented tests over broad refactors.
- Keep production changes minimal unless a testability seam is genuinely needed.
- Measure against the existing Rust crate(s) in this repository.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Coverage for the existing codebase reaches at least 90% on the chosen measurement tool.
2. New tests cover the highest-risk untested branches and error paths.
3. The test suite continues to pass with no new lint or doc failures.

## Notes
- Start from the current codebase as-is; do not add unrelated features.
- Use the repo's existing testing style and keep the changes localized.
close_reason: Raised coverage to 90%+

title: Permission gate: session/request_permission + allow_always memory
description:
## Goal
Gate mutating tools behind explicit user approval.

## Scope
- In: before any mutating tool, call session/request_permission offering allow_once/allow_always/reject_once/reject_always; proceed only on allow; remember allow_always per-tool per-session; read-only tools never prompt.
- Out: mode posture (next task), the mutating tools themselves.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A mutating tool in default posture prompts before executing and respects the chosen option.
2. allow_always suppresses re-prompts for that tool for the session; reject aborts the tool with a result fed back to the model.

## Testing
- Fake client returning each option kind; assert gating + allow_always memory + reject handling.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop. Core M2 infra.
close_reason: Permission gate core infra implemented and verified

title: M1: Read-only tool loop
description:
## Problem Statement

A chat-only agent cannot investigate a codebase. The model must read files and search before it can help.

## Solution

Add the agentic tool loop with read-only tools: read_file (via client fs when advertised, else local), list_dir/glob/grep (local), each reported through tool_call/tool_call_update updates. No permission prompts (read-only).

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want the agent to read files, so that its answers reflect my actual code.
2. As a user, I want the agent to search/glob/list the project, so that it locates relevant code on its own.
3. As a user, I want each tool call shown in my editor, so that I can see what the agent is doing.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given DeepSeek returns tool_calls for read-only tools, when the loop runs, then each is reported as tool_call (pending) then tool_call_update (completed) and the result is fed back to the model.
2. Given read_file and client advertises fs.readTextFile, then the read goes through fs/read_text_file (respecting unsaved buffers); otherwise a local read.
3. Given grep/glob/list_dir, then they execute locally (ripgrep engine / globset) and return bounded results.
4. Given the model issues further tool calls, then the loop iterates until none remain, or the iteration cap (default ~25) trips returning StopReason::max_turn_requests.

## Implementation Notes

- Tool JSON-schema registry passed in the DeepSeek tools param; accumulate streamed tool-call arguments by index.
- Search via ignore + grep crates; glob via globset.
- Capability-driven routing of read_file (client vs local).

## Testing Notes

- Mock model emitting tool calls; assert the tool_call/tool_call_update sequence and history feedback. Test the max_turn_requests cap.

## Out of Scope

Mutating tools, permissions, shell.
close_reason: Read-only tool loop implemented and verified

title: Support ACP v1 MCP stdio servers
description:
ACP v1 Session Setup says clients may include mcpServers in session/new and all agents MUST support connecting to MCP servers via stdio. The adapter currently stores cwd/additionalDirectories but does not consume request.mcp_servers or connect MCP servers.

Implement support for stdio MCP servers supplied in session/new. The implementation should keep external dependencies out of domain logic, avoid leaking secrets in logs, and expose MCP tools to the model/tool loop as appropriate.

Evidence:
- deepseek-acp-adapter registers session/new only and handle_new_session_request ignores MCP server configs in src/main.rs.
- ACP v1 session setup requires stdio MCP support for agents.
- codex-acp initializes MCP-related capabilities and parses supplied MCP servers as a reference.

Acceptance criteria:
- session/new accepts stdio MCP server definitions and starts/connects them for the session.
- MCP tool results can participate in the prompt tool loop or an explicit unsupported path is returned per tool.
- Tests cover stdio MCP configuration parsing, launch failure, and successful tool exposure.
close_reason: Implemented stdio MCP session connection, tool exposure, execution routing, and tests

title: Advertise additionalDirectories session capability
description:
Audit finding: handle_new_session_request validates and stores additionalDirectories and tools use them in relative path resolution, but build_initialize_response does not advertise sessionCapabilities.additionalDirectories. ACP v1 says clients MUST only send additionalDirectories when the agent advertises this capability.

Acceptance criteria:
- Advertise sessionCapabilities.additionalDirectories if the adapter intends to support additional workspace roots.
- Ensure cwd remains the primary root and relative paths resolve as documented.
- Bound write/edit/run operations to the effective root set or explicitly document unrestricted behavior.
- Tests cover capability advertisement and additional root path resolution.

title: Restore max_turn_requests guard in prompt loop
description:
Audit finding: run_prompt_turn loops until the model stops requesting tools, but ACP v1 defines StopReason::max_turn_requests for exceeding the maximum number of model requests in a single turn. The previous MAX_TURN_REQUESTS guard is absent in current src/main.rs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Prompt turns have a bounded maximum model/tool loop count.
- When exceeded, session/prompt returns StopReason::MaxTurnRequests.
- Existing tool-call replay behavior still works below the limit.
- Tests cover the limit boundary and the stop reason.
close_reason: Restored MAX_TURN_REQUESTS guard and regression coverage for StopReason::MaxTurnRequests

title: M4: Model selection, image input & docs polish
description:
## Problem Statement

v0.1 runs a fixed model, handles text+files only, and first-run setup is non-obvious (GUI editors may not inherit DEEPSEEK_API_KEY from the shell). Polish for real-world use.

## Solution

Wire the unstable session/set_model + availableModels (v4-flash / v4-pro / v4-pro+thinking) for capable clients with DEEPSEEK_MODEL as the default; map ACP image content blocks to DeepSeek image_url parts; write the README including the GUI env-inheritance gotcha.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed user, I want to switch models mid-session, so that I can trade cost/quality per task.
2. As a CodeCompanion user, I want a sensible default model from env, so that it works without a picker.
3. As a user, I want to send images, so that the model can use screenshots/diagrams.
4. As a new user, I want clear setup docs, so that DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is found even when launched from a GUI editor.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a client supporting set_model, then availableModels are advertised and session/set_model switches the active model; otherwise DEEPSEEK_MODEL (default deepseek-v4-pro) is used.
2. Given an ACP prompt with image content, then it is forwarded as an OpenAI-style image_url content part to DeepSeek.
3. Given the README, then it documents env-only config and the agent_servers.env workaround for GUI-launched editors.

## Implementation Notes

- set_model lives behind the crate's unstable feature; a graceful no-op for clients lacking it.

## Testing Notes

- set_model present vs absent; image content mapping.

## Out of Scope

Auto model-router, MCP client support, loadSession.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: write_file tool (client fs, permission-gated)
description:
## Goal
Let the model create/overwrite files, shown as a native diff.

## Scope
- In: write_file(path, content) via client fs/write_text_file (creates/overwrites); permission-gated; reported via tool_call_update.
- Out: targeted string edits (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. write_file writes through the client fs (editor renders the diff) after permission.
2. Failure (e.g., no fs.writeTextFile capability) returns a clear tool error fed back to the model.

## Testing
- Fake client capturing writes; assert content, permission gating, error path.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate.
close_reason: Implemented permission-gated write_file via client fs; covered by tests

title: Permission-posture modes (ask/accept-edits/yolo) + session/set_mode
description:
## Goal
Let the user trade safety for speed via modes.

## Scope
- In: advertise modes in session/new (ask=default, accept-edits, yolo) with currentModeId/availableModes; session/set_mode switches live; mode drives the permission gate (accept-edits auto-allows edits but prompts shell; yolo auto-allows all).
- Out: model selection (M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Modes are advertised; session/set_mode changes posture mid-session.
2. accept-edits auto-allows edits, still prompts shell; yolo auto-allows all; ask prompts all mutations.

## Testing
- Matrix: each mode x {edit, shell} asserts prompt-or-not.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate.
close_reason: Session modes and live mode switching implemented and verified

title: ACP stdio connection & initialize handshake
description:
## Goal
Wire the ACP Agent over stdio and implement the initialize handshake.

## Scope
- In: AgentSideConnection over stdio (agent-client-protocol + -tokio); impl initialize (version negotiate; advertise agent caps: no auth, promptCaps text+embeddedContext+image, loadSession=false); store client capabilities; authenticate as no-op.
- Out: sessions, prompts, tools.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given an initialize request, the agent negotiates protocol version and returns the advertised capabilities.
2. Client capabilities (fs/terminal) are parsed and stored for later routing.

## Testing
- In-memory fake ACP client sends initialize; assert negotiated version + caps; client caps stored.

## Notes
- Translation boundary: ACP types stay at this layer. Depends on skeleton.
close_reason: Implemented stdio agent bootstrap and initialize handshake

title: Image input: map ACP image blocks to DeepSeek image_url
description:
## Goal
Let users send images to the model.

## Scope
- In: map ACP image content blocks in a prompt to OpenAI-style image_url content parts in the DeepSeek request.
- Out: image generation/output.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A prompt containing an image block produces a DeepSeek request with a correctly-formatted image_url part.

## Testing
- Unit: ACP image block -> request payload mapping.

## Notes
- Depends on the M0 prompt turn. DeepSeek V4 supports vision.
close_reason: Implemented in the current patchset and verified by tests/docs

title: Handle non-UTF-8 file reads without internal ACP error
description:
When using `deepseek_acp_adapter`, a read-file tool call against a Cargo target fingerprint artifact fails as an internal ACP/JSON-RPC error instead of returning a controlled tool error.

Observed log excerpt:

```text
2026-06-04T06:29:21.735774Z DEBUG connection{name="deepseek-acp-adapter"}: agent_client_protocol::jsonrpc::outgoing_actor: send_raw_message Notification { untyped: UntypedMessage { method: "session/update", params: Object {"sessionId": String("session-87b8ba86-3228-48c5-8660-b15e04c14b9f"), "update": Object {"kind": String("read"), "rawInput": Object {"path": String("target/debug/.fingerprint/agent-client-protocol-077e92246336e4d0/dep-lib-agent_client_protocol")}, "sessionUpdate": String("tool_call"), "title": String("read_file"), "toolCallId": String("call_00_LH4DZ1YxP8fpuPD71CBj3477")}} } } tx=UnboundedSender { closed: false }
2026-06-04T06:29:21.735831Z DEBUG connection{name="deepseek-acp-adapter"}: agent_client_protocol::jsonrpc::outgoing_actor: outgoing_protocol_actor Notification { untyped: UntypedMessage { method: "session/update", params: Object {"sessionId": String("session-87b8ba86-3228-48c5-8660-b15e04c14b9f"), "update": Object {"kind": String("read"), "rawInput": Object {"path": String("target/debug/.fingerprint/agent-client-protocol-077e92246336e4d0/dep-lib-agent_client_protocol")}, "sessionUpdate": String("tool_call"), "title": String("read_file"), "toolCallId": String("call_00_LH4DZ1YxP8fpuPD71CBj3477")}} } }
2026-06-04T06:29:21.735868Z DEBUG connection{name="deepseek-acp-adapter"}: agent_client_protocol::jsonrpc::outgoing_actor: outgoing_protocol_actor Request { id: String("51b88be8-e5ea-46b9-ab8f-dd922ac39597"), method: "fs/read_text_file", role_id: Singleton("agent_client_protocol::role::acp::Client", TypeId(0x5550ea44545b2b99be47548edd5c74c4)), untyped: UntypedMessage { method: "fs/read_text_file", params: Object {"limit": Number(200), "line": Number(1), "path": String("/home/lotso/code/deepseek-acp-adapter/target/debug/.fingerprint/agent-client-protocol-077e92246336e4d0/dep-lib-agent_client_protocol"), "sessionId": String("session-87b8ba86-3228-48c5-8660-b15e04c14b9f")} }, response_tx: Sender { complete: false } }
error: Internal error: {
  "data": "stream did not contain valid UTF-8",
  "spawned_at": "/home/lotso/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/agent-client-protocol-0.13.1/src/jsonrpc.rs:1245:39"
}
```

Expected behavior:
- Non-UTF-8/binary files should not surface as an internal JSON-RPC error.
- The adapter should either reject the read with a clear, sanitized tool error or avoid issuing `fs/read_text_file` for likely binary artifacts.
- Session/tool-call state should remain consistent after the failed read.

Notes:
- The problematic path is under `target/debug/.fingerprint/.../dep-lib-agent_client_protocol`.
- The ACP request method is `fs/read_text_file`, with `limit: 200` and `line: 1`.
close_reason: Handled non-UTF-8 read_file inputs as failed tool results instead of raw internal ACP errors
